body: this PR is a tentative solution to #4077 and changes the default time indexing setting to `Clamp` This MWE from #4077 ```julia # First we generate some test data and write to disk grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(2, 2, 2), extent=(1, 1, 1)) times = 0:0.1:3 filename = "MWE_data_file.jld2" f_tmp = Field{Center,Center,Center}(grid) f = FieldTimeSeries{Center, Center, Center}(grid, times; backend = OnDisk(), path = filename, name = "f") for (it, time) in enumerate(f.times) set!(f_tmp, (x, y, z) -> sin(2* π * time /3) ) set!(f,f_tmp,it) end # Now we load the FTS partly in memory N_in_mem = 5 f_fts = FieldTimeSeries(filename, "f"; backend = InMemory(N_in_mem)) f_fts2 = FieldTimeSeries(filename, "f"; backend = InMemory(N_in_mem), time_indexing = Cyclical()) f_fts3 = FieldTimeSeries(filename, "f"; backend = InMemory(N_in_mem), time_indexing = Clamp()) s1 = [] s2 = [] s3 = [] for i in 0:0.01:10 push!(s1, f_fts[Time(i)][1, 1, 1]) push!(s2, f_fts2[Time(i)][1, 1, 1]) push!(s3, f_fts3[Time(i)][1, 1, 1]) end ``` yields Comments are welcome TODO: - [x] add interpolation and indexing tests
title: Fix some `FieldTimeSeries` bugs

body:
This PR implements a `RotatedLatitudeLongitudeGrid`, which has the structure of a `LatitudeLongitudeGrid` but with a displaced pole.

There are two ways to implement this. One is to make a LatitudeLongitudeGrid, change the latitudes, and then write the data down to `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid`. The pole displacement is then encoded in the conformal mapping of the grid. Another is to actually embed the coordinate shift into `φnode`. I think the former is probably a simpler approach. The latter saves a little bit of memory though.

This PR moves some files around and implements the basic abstraction. Next we have to write the metrics into `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid` and add tests.

cc @simone-silvestri
title: Implement RotatedLatitudeLongitudeGrid
